Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 93 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Teaching Claude to QA a mobile app 56 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 153 upvotes, 33 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 146 upvotes, 32 comments
  5. How to Attract AI Bots to Your Open Source Project 52 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 344 upvotes, 97 comments
  7. 25 Years of Eggs 241 upvotes, 69 comments
  8. Palantir extends reach into British state as gets access to sensitive FCA data 165 upvotes, 46 comments
  9. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 105 upvotes, 28 comments
  10. A review of dice that came with the white castle 123 upvotes, 36 comments
  11. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 289 upvotes, 101 comments
  12. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 133 upvotes, 47 comments
  13. Vectorization of Verilog Designs and its Effects on Verification and Synthesis 19 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 133 upvotes, 50 comments
  15. Theodosian Land Walls of Constantinople 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 249 upvotes, 118 comments
  17. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 140 upvotes, 66 comments
  18. Diverse perspectives on AI from Rust contributors and maintainers 89 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. LLMs Predict My Coffee 56 upvotes, 23 comments
  20. The future of version control 367 upvotes, 215 comments
  21. OpenClaw is a security nightmare dressed up as a daydream 275 upvotes, 191 comments
  22. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 363 upvotes, 261 comments
  23. Why I love NixOS 159 upvotes, 126 comments
  24. Show HN: Codala, a social network built on scanning barcodes 22 upvotes, 11 comments
  25. Iran war energy crisis is a renewable energy wake-up call 72 upvotes, 55 comments
  26. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 207 upvotes, 193 comments
  27. First and Lego Education Partnership Update 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. Windows native app development is a mess 308 upvotes, 327 comments
  29. They're Vibe-Coding Spam Now 15 upvotes, 9 comments
  30. What Young Workers Are Doing to AI-Proof Themselves 52 upvotes, 53 comments

Last generated 8:00am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
